---
title: "Hermes"
description: "NousResearch's CLI agent on Bedrock via bedrockify."
---

<Note>
**Experimental pack.** Hermes is NousResearch's CLI agent. This pack runs it against Bedrock through the bedrockify proxy (OpenAI-compatible API over localhost), so you get Hermes's UX with Claude/Nova/etc. as the backing model.
</Note>

## When to use

- Want Hermes's terminal-focused workflow with self-improving skills
- Want Bedrock billing but Hermes's CLI
- Lighter-weight than OpenClaw — no gateway, no memory service

## What the pack installs

- `bedrockify` dep on port 8090 (OpenAI-compatible Bedrock proxy)
- Hermes CLI (from NousResearch's installer)
- Env config pointing Hermes at `http://localhost:8090/v1` with the configured model

## Install — non-interactive

```bash
curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
  --pack hermes \
  --profile builder
```

## Pack parameters

| Param | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `region` | `us-east-1` | AWS region for Bedrock |
| `hermes-model` | `anthropic/claude-opus-4.6` | Model ID (OpenAI-style via bedrockify). Generic `--model` is ignored. |
| `bedrockify-port` | `8090` | Where bedrockify listens |

<Info>
Hermes uses OpenAI-style model IDs (`anthropic/claude-...`), not Bedrock IDs (`us.anthropic.*`). Bedrockify does the translation.
</Info>

## Resource requirements

| | All profiles |
|---|---|
| Instance | `t4g.medium` |
| Root volume | 40 GB |
| Data volume | 0 GB |

## First-run

```bash
aws ssm start-session --target <instance-id>
hermes --version
hermes
```

Bedrock auth is via the instance IAM role; bedrockify handles the proxy transparently.

## Tear-down

Delete the stack.
